title: Rosalind Franklin's Beautiful Twist
titleZh: 罗莎琳德·富兰克林美丽的双螺旋

q.en: In what year and country was Rosalind Elsie Franklin born?
q.zh: 罗莎琳德·埃尔西·富兰克林出生在哪一年、哪个国家？

options.en: In 1920, in England.
In 1900, in France.
In 1945, in the United States.
In 1920, in Germany.

explain.en: The book states, 'In 1920, Rosalind Elsie Franklin was born in England.'
explain.zh: 书中写道：'1920年，罗莎琳德·埃尔西·富兰克林出生在英格兰。'

q.en: What scientific method did Rosalind become an expert in at Cambridge?
q.zh: 在剑桥时，罗莎琳德成为了哪种科学方法的专家？

options.en: X-ray crystallography, using X-rays to examine molecules.
options.zh: X射线晶体学，即用X射线研究分子结构。

options.en: Building large metal models of atoms.
options.zh: 搭建大型金属原子模型。

options.en: Writing newspaper articles about science.
Performing surgery on patients.

explain.en: The book says she 'became an expert in X-ray crystallography, a way of using X-rays to examine molecules.'
explain.zh: 书中说她'成了X射线晶体学专家，即用X射线研究分子的方法'。

q.en: What was Photograph 51, which Rosalind took in 1952?
q.zh: 罗莎琳德在1952年拍摄的'51号照片'是什么？

options.en: The most amazing X-ray picture of DNA that anyone had ever taken.
options.zh: 当时人们拍过的最惊人、最清晰的DNAX射线照片。

options.en: A photo of her favorite mountain.
options.zh: 一张她最爱的大山的照片。

options.en: A picture of King's College where she worked.
options.zh: 她工作的国王学院的照片。

options.en: A self-portrait for her laboratory ID.
options.zh: 她实验室证件的自拍照。

explain.en: In 1952 she took 'Photograph 51, the most amazing picture of DNA that anyone had ever taken.'
explain.zh: 1952年她拍下了'51号照片，那是当时任何人拍过的最惊人的DNA照片'。

q.en: How did James Watson and Francis Crick learn the shape of DNA?
q.zh: 詹姆斯·沃森和弗朗西斯·克里克是如何得知DNA形状的？

options.en: Maurice Wilkins showed them Rosalind's Photograph 51 without her knowledge, and they built a model from it.
options.zh: 莫里斯·威尔金斯在罗莎琳德不知情的情况下给他们看了51号照片，他们据此建了模型。

options.en: Rosalind gave them the photo willingly and helped them build the model.
options.zh: 罗莎琳德主动把照片给他们，并帮忙建模型。

options.en: They guessed the shape without looking at any of her work.
options.zh: 他们没看她的任何成果，纯靠猜。

options.en: They read it from a book Rosalind had published.
options.zh: 他们从罗莎琳德出版的书里读到的。

explain.en: Maurice Wilkins showed Jim Photograph 51 'when Rosalind wasn't looking,' and Watson and Crick used it to build the double-helix model without asking her.
explain.zh: 莫里斯·威尔金斯'在罗莎琳德没注意时'给吉姆看了51号照片，沃森和克里克未经她同意就用它建出了双螺旋模型。

q.en: Why did Rosalind Franklin never win the Nobel Prize for the DNA discovery?
q.zh: 为什么罗莎琳德·富兰克林没能因发现DNA而获得诺贝尔奖？

options.en: She died in 1958 at age 37, and only living people can win the Nobel Prize; Watson, Crick, and Wilkins won in 1962.
options.zh: 她1958年去世，年仅37岁，而诺贝尔奖只颁给在世的人；沃森、克里克和威尔金斯于1962年获奖。

options.en: She refused to accept the prize when offered.
She was not a scientist, only a lab assistant.
options.zh: 她不是科学家，只是实验室助理。

options.en: The Nobel committee had never heard of her work.
options.zh: 诺贝尔奖委员会从没听说过她的研究。

explain.en: Four years after her 1958 death, Watson, Crick, and Maurice won the 1962 Nobel Prize; 'only living people can win the Nobel Prize.'
explain.zh: 她1958年去世四年后，沃森、克里克和莫里斯获得了1962年诺贝尔奖；'只有活着的人才能获诺贝尔奖'。

q.en: Which activity did Rosalind enjoy most and find most relaxing?
q.zh: 罗莎琳德最喜欢、觉得最放松的活动是什么？

options.en: Mountain climbing in other countries.
Playing the piano.
Painting portraits.
Swimming in the ocean.

explain.en: The book says her favorite activity was mountain climbing and 'she found nothing more relaxing than traveling to other countries to climb new mountains.'
explain.zh: 书中说她最爱登山，'她觉得最放松的事莫过于去其他国家爬新山'。

q.en: At Cambridge University, what was the ratio of men to women students?
q.zh: 在剑桥大学，男女学生的比例是多少？

options.en: For every nine men admitted, only one woman was allowed to enroll.
options.zh: 每录取9名男生，只许1名女生入学。

options.en: There were equal numbers of men and women.
Only women were admitted, no men.
There were two men for every one woman.

explain.en: The book states, 'For every nine men admitted to the university, only one woman was allowed to enroll.'
explain.zh: 书中说：'大学每录取9名男生，只许1名女生入学。'

q.en: After King's College, where did Rosalind go and what did she study there?
q.zh: 离开国王学院后，罗莎琳德去了哪里、研究什么？

options.en: Birkbeck College, where she led a team studying viruses.
options.zh: 去了伯贝克学院，带领团队研究病毒。

options.en: A hospital, where she trained to be a nurse.
France, where she stopped doing science.
A school, where she taught PE.

explain.en: At Birkbeck College she 'was the boss, with a team of talented and respectful scientists working for her. They studied viruses.'
explain.zh: 在伯贝克学院，她'是负责人，带领一群才华横溢、尊重她的科学家，研究病毒'。

q.en: In this book, what does the term 'double helix' mean?
q.zh: 在这本书中，'double helix'（双螺旋）是什么意思？

options.en: A spiral made up of two strands — the shape of DNA.
options.zh: 由两条链组成的螺旋——即DNA的形状。

options.en: A kind of X-ray machine.
A type of mountain range.
A medical term for cancer.

explain.en: The glossary defines 'double helix' as 'a spiral made up of two strands,' which is the shape of DNA.
explain.zh: 词汇表把 double helix 定义为'由两条链组成的螺旋'，也就是DNA的形状。

q.en: What is the author's main purpose in writing this book?
q.zh: 作者写这本书的主要目的是什么？

options.en: To inform readers about Rosalind Franklin's life and her key role in discovering the structure of DNA.
options.zh: 向读者介绍罗莎琳德·富兰克林的生平，以及她在发现DNA结构中的关键作用。

options.en: To teach readers how to climb mountains.
To tell a made-up fairy tale.
To convince readers to become doctors.

explain.en: The book is a biography explaining her determination and 'one of the most important discoveries in the history of science' — DNA's double helix.
explain.zh: 本书是传记，讲述她的决心以及'科学史上最重要的发现之一'——DNA双螺旋。

prompt.en: Rosalind Franklin took the famous Photograph 51, but Watson and Crick used it without her knowledge and became famous. Who do you think deserves more credit for discovering the shape of DNA, and why?
prompt.zh: 罗莎琳德·富兰克林拍出了著名的51号照片，但沃森和克里克在瞒着她的情况下用了它，并因此成名。你认为谁更该为发现DNA形状而获得荣誉？为什么？

en: Remember that Maurice Wilkins showed Jim Watson Photograph 51 when Rosalind wasn't looking, and they built their model from it.
Think about Watson's later admission that he and Crick 'couldn't have understood DNA without Rosalind.'
zh: 记住莫里斯·威尔金斯在罗莎琳德没注意时给沃森看了51号照片，他们据此建立模型。
想想沃森后来承认：他和克里克'若没有罗莎琳德，就不可能弄懂DNA'。
